claims.flip_evidence_text: (i) The representations and warranties of the Group Companies set forth in ... the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure)... shall have been true and correct... (except, with respect to... the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c), for any de minimis inaccuracies)... (ii) all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects...
claims.notes: The claim conflates two distinct standards: the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c) are held to a 'true and correct' standard with a de minimis exception (not 'in all material respects'), while all other portions of Section 4.2(c) are held to an 'all material respects' standard without a de minimis exception.

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
Section 4.2 Capital Structure. <omitted> (c) <omitted> All outstanding shares of capital stock or other equity interests of the Subsidiaries of each Group Company are owned by either Group Company, or a direct or indirect wholly -16 owned Subsidiary of such Group Company, are free and clear of all Encumbrances, other than Permitted Encumbrances, and are duly authorized, validly issued, fully paid and nonassessable. <omitted> Section 4.3 Authority; No Violations; Consents and Approvals. (a) Each Group Company has all requisite corporate power and authority to execute and deliver this Agreement and to perform its obligations hereunder. The execution and delivery of this Agreement by each Group Company and the consummation by each Group Company of the Transactions have been duly authorized by all necessary corporate action on the part of such Group Company, subject, only with respect to the consummation of the Merger, the Company Stockholder Approval and the filing of the Certificate of First Merger and Certificate of Second Merger with the Office of the Secretary of State of the State of Delaware. This Agreement has been duly executed and delivered by each Group Company and, assuming the due and valid execution of this Agreement by Parent and the Merger Subs, constitutes a valid and binding obligation of each Group Company enforceable against such Group Company in accordance with its terms, subject, as to enforceability, to bankruptcy, insolvency, reorganization, moratorium and other Laws of general applicability relating to or affecting creditors’ rights and to general principles of equity regardless of whether such enforceability is considered in a Proceeding in equity or at Law (collectively, “Creditors’ Rights”). The Company Board, at a meeting duly called and held, has by unanimous vote (A) determined that this Agreement and the Transactions, including the Merger, are fair and reasonable to, and advisable and in the best interests of, the Company and the holders of Company Common Stock, (B) approved and declared advisable this Agreement and the consummation of the Transactions <omitted> Section 7.2 Additional Conditions to Obligations of Parent and the Merger Subs. The obligations of Parent, Merger Sub Inc. and Merger Sub LLC to consummate the Merger are subject to the satisfaction at or prior to the Effective Time of the following conditions, any or all of which may be waived exclusively by Parent, in whole or in part, to the extent permitted by applicable Law: (a) Representations and Warranties of the Company. (i) The representations and warranties of the Group Companies set forth in the first sentence of Section 4.1 (Organization, Standing and Power), Section 4.2(a) (Capital Structure), Section 4.2(b) (Capital Structure), the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure), Section 4.3(a) (Authority), and Section 4.6(a) (Absence of Certain Changes or Events) shall have been true and correct as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct as of the Closing Date, as though made on and as of the Closing Date (except, with respect to Section 4.2(a) and the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c), for any de minimis inaccuracies and Section 4.2(b) due to the conversion of the Holdings Class B Units pursuant to Section 3.2) (except that representations and warranties that speak as of a specified date or period of time shall have been true and correct only as of such date or period of time), (ii) all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects as of the date of 76 this Agreement and shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, as though made on and as of the Closing Date (except that representations and warranties that speak as of a specified date or period of time shall have been true and correct in all material respects only as of such date or period of time), and (iii) all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Article IV shall have been true and correct as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct as of the Closing Date, as though made on and as of the Closing Date (except that representations and warranties that speak as of a specified date or period of time shall have been true and correct only as of such date or period of time), except, in the case of this clause (iii), where the failure of such representations and warranties to be so true and correct (without regard to qualification or exceptions contained therein as to “materiality,” “in all material respects” or “Company Material Adverse Effect”) would not reasonably be expected to have, individually or in the aggregate, a Company Material Adverse Effect. (Pages 80-81)

Question:
Accuracy of Target "General" R&W: Bringdown Standard Answer

Llama answer:
The representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, except for any de minimis inaccuracies.

Canonical claims:
- claim-f1fa5f8bcd21: The representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, except for any de minimis inaccuracies.

message.reasoning:
The user wants me to evaluate a claim against a contract excerpt, a question, and a model answer (Llama answer).

 Let me analyze the contract excerpt carefully.

 The relevant part is Section 7.2(a) about conditions to obligations of Parent and Merger Subs.

 It says:
 (i) The representations and warranties of the Group Companies set forth in ... the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure), ... shall have been true and correct as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct as of the Closing Date ... (except, with respect to Section 4.2(a) and the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c), for any de minimis inaccuracies and Section 4.2(b) due to the conversion ...)

 (ii) all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date ...

 So for Section 4.2(c):
 - The third and fifth sentences have a standard of "true and correct" with an exception for "de minimis inaccuracies"
 - All OTHER representations and warranties in Section 4.2(c) have a standard of "true and correct in all material respects"

 The claim is: "The representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, except for any de minimis inaccuracies."

 This claim says ALL of Section 4.2(c) is subject to "all material respects" AND "except for any de minimis inaccuracies".

 But the contract says:
 - Third and fifth sentences: true and correct (not "in all material respects") except for de minimis inaccuracies
 - Other parts of 4.2(c): true and correct in all material respects (no de minimis exception mentioned for these)

 Wait, let me re-read carefully.

 Section 7.2(a)(i): "...the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure)... shall have been true and correct... (except, with respect to Section 4.2(a) and the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c), for any de minimis inaccuracies...)"

 Section 7.2(a)(ii): "all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects..."

 So:
 - Third and fifth sentences of 4.2(c): "true and correct" (not "in all material respects") with de minimis exception
 - Other parts of 4.2(c): "true and correct in all material respects" (no de minimis exception)

 The claim states that ALL of Section 4.2(c) shall be "true and correct in all material respects" with "except for any de minimis inaccuracies".

 This is partially true because:
 - It correctly identifies that some parts have de minimis exceptions and some have material respects, but it incorrectly applies "in all material respects" to the entire section including the third and fifth sentences, and it incorrectly applies the de minimis exception to the entire section (the "all other" parts in clause (ii) don't have the de minimis exception).

 Actually, wait. The claim says the representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) shall have been true and correct in all material respects... except for any de minimis inaccuracies.

 But the contract treats different sentences within 4.2(c) differently. The third and fifth sentences are held to a "true and correct" (not material respects) standard with de minimis exception. The other sentences in 4.2(c) are held to "true and correct in all material respects" without the de minimis exception.

 So the claim is partially true because it conflates two different standards that apply to different portions of Section 4.2(c).

 However, looking at the Llama answer, it seems to be answering the question about the bringdown standard for Section 4.2(c). The Llama answer says: "The representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, except for any de minimis inaccuracies."

 This is not fully accurate because it doesn't distinguish between the third/fifth sentences and the rest of 4.2(c).

 But wait, is the claim exactly matching the Llama answer? Yes, the claim is the same text as the Llama answer.

 The question is: "Accuracy of Target 'General' R&W: Bringdown Standard Answer"

 The user wants me to evaluate the canonical claim against the contract.

 Let me think about correctness_label:
 - Is the claim true? No, because Section 4.2(c) is not uniformly subject to both "all material respects" and "de minimis inaccuracies". The third and fifth sentences have a different standard.
 - Is it false? Not entirely, because for the "other" parts of 4.2(c), it is "all material respects", and for the third/fifth sentences, there is a de minimis exception (though without "all material respects").
 - So it's partially_true.

 Load bearing: Does the claim contain a specific assertion that, if flipped/changed, would alter the legal meaning in a way that contradicts the contract? Yes, the claim asserts a unified standard for all of 4.2(c). The key differences are:
 1. "in all material respects" applies to all of 4.2(c) - but for third/fifth sentences, the contract says "true and correct" (not "in all material respects")
 2. "except for any de minimis inaccuracies" applies to all of 4.2(c) - but for the "other" parts, the contract doesn't mention de minimis inaccuracies

 Actually, looking more carefully at the contract:
 - (i) covers: first sentence of 4.1, 4.2(a), 4.2(b), third and fifth sentences of 4.2(c), 4.3(a), 4.6(a). These are held to "true and correct" (not material respects), with specific exceptions: de minimis for 4.2(a) and third/fifth sentences of 4.2(c); and 4.2(b) due to conversion.
 - (ii) covers: all OTHER representations in Section 4.2(c). These are held to "true and correct in all material respects".
 - (iii) covers: all other Article IV reps. These are held to "true and correct" except where failure wouldn't reasonably be expected to have CMAE.

 So for the third and fifth sentences of 4.2(c): standard is "true and correct" (not "in all material respects") with de minimis exception.
 For all other parts of 4.2(c): standard is "true and correct in all material respects" with no de minimis exception.

 The claim says ALL of 4.2(c) is "true and correct in all material respects... except for any de minimis inaccuracies."

 This is a simplification that is not fully accurate. It's partially true.

 Now, load_bearing_label: Is this claim load-bearing? A claim is load-bearing if it captures a specific legal standard or condition that is central to the analysis. Here, the claim is asserting the bringdown standard for 4.2(c). The specific qualifiers ("in all material respects" and "de minimis inaccuracies") are definitely load-bearing because they determine the threshold for closing conditions.

 If we flip the evidence text to show what the contract actually says, it would highlight that the claim oversimplifies by not distinguishing between the third/fifth sentences and the rest.

 Wait, I need to determine if the claim is "load-bearing" in the sense that it's a central assertion that the model answer makes. Yes, this is the core of the Llama answer.

 For flip_evidence_text: I should provide the text from the contract that shows the correct distinction. The relevant text is from Section 7.2(a)(i) and (ii).

 Let me draft the flip evidence:
 "The representations and warranties of the Group Companies set forth in ... the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure)... shall have been true and correct... (except, with respect to... the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c), for any de minimis inaccuracies)... all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects..."

 Actually, the flip evidence should be the text that contradicts or nuances the claim. Since the claim says the ENTIRE Section 4.2(c) is subject to BOTH "all material respects" AND "de minimis inaccuracies", the flip evidence should show that:
 1. The third and fifth sentences are subject to de minimis but NOT "all material respects"
 2. The other parts are subject to "all material respects" but NOT de minimis

 So the flip evidence text could be:
 "(i) The representations and warranties of the Group Companies set forth in ... the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure)... shall have been true and correct... (except, with respect to... the third and fifth sentences of Section 4.2(c), for any de minimis inaccuracies)... (ii) all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects..."

 This shows the split standard.

 Let me reconsider correctness_label. Is it possible the claim is considered "false" because it misstates the standard for the entire section? The instructions say to use true, false, or partially_true.

 Given that the claim captures the standard for some of 4.2(c) (the "other" parts) but adds an exception (de minimis) that doesn't apply to those parts, and applies "all material respects" to parts where it doesn't apply (third/fifth sentences), it's partially true. It gets some things right but conflates two different standards.

 Actually, looking at the claim again: "The representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c) (Capital Structure) shall have been true and correct in all material respects as of the date of this Agreement and shall be true and correct in all material respects as of the Closing Date, except for any de minimis inaccuracies."

 This is literally what the Llama answer says. The question asks about the "General" R&W bringdown standard. But Section 4.2(c) is not a "general" R&W in the sense of being a general representation; it's a specific representation about capital structure. However, within Section 4.2(c), there are different sentences with different standards.

 I think "partially_true" is the right label.

 For load_bearing: yes, because this is the main substantive claim in the answer about the bringdown standard.

 For flip_evidence_text: I'll provide the relevant contract language showing the bifurcated treatment.

 Let me check if there's any other nuance. The claim says "representations and warranties of the Company" while the contract in 7.2(a)(i) says "representations and warranties of the Group Companies" for the listed sections. But in (ii) it says "representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c)". Wait, let me check:

 (i) "The representations and warranties of the Group Companies set forth in..."
 (ii) "all other representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c)..."

 Hmm, interesting. (i) refers to Group Companies, (ii) refers to Company. But the claim says "representations and warranties of the Company set forth in Section 4.2(c)". This matches (ii) but not (i). However, the third and fifth sentences of 4.2(c) are in (i) which refers to Group Companies. But the claim says "Company". Is this significant? The contract uses "Group Companies" in (i) and "Company" in (ii). But the claim uses "Company" for all of 4.2(c). This might be another inaccuracy, though perhaps minor if Group Companies and Company are used somewhat interchangeably in context. Actually, looking at the contract, Section 4.2(c) is under Article IV which is the Company's representations. The "Group Companies" in (i) might be because those specific reps are made by Group Companies. But the claim says "Company". This is likely a minor terminological issue.

 The main issue is the conflation of standards.

 Let me format the output as requested.
